nodes.en: Rituals and ceremonies are formalized practices marking significant cultural, spiritual, or lifecycle moments—birth, coming of age, marriage, death. They externalize internal transitions and reinforce community identity.
nodes.cs: Rituály a ceremoniály jsou formalizované praktiky označující významné kulturní, spirituální nebo životní momenty—narození, dospělost, sňatek, smrt. Externalizují vnitřní přechody a posilují identitu komunity.
nodes.brief:
Rituals and ceremonies are formalized practices that mark significant cultural, spiritual, or lifecycle moments — birth, coming of age, marriage, death — externalizing internal transitions and reinforcing community identity through prescribed sequence and symbol.

The branch shares its festive and performative register with [[10.07]] Games & Recreation, where contest or play is folded into a rite rather than staged as its own tradition, and with [[10.05]] Music & Songs, whose repertoires are frequently composed specifically for ceremonial use and are meaningless outside it. It is distinct from [[10.02]] Folklore & Mythology, which supplies the belief system a ceremony often enacts rather than the enactment itself. All four sit under [[10]] Cultural Preservation.

UNESCO's [Social Practices, Rituals and Festive Events](https://ich.unesco.org/en/social-practices-rituals-and-festive-events-00055) domain is the standard reference for classifying and safeguarding this material, and the [Human Relations Area Files](https://hraf.yale.edu/) cross-cultural database indexes ceremonial practice across documented societies.
